problem:
Find the point on the line defined by
\[\begin{pmatrix} 4 \\ 0 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix} + t \begin{pmatrix} -2 \\ 6 \\ -3 \end{pmatrix}\]that is closest to the point $(2,3,4).$

classified_questions.question: What is the equation of the line defined by the vectors \(\begin{pmatrix} 4 \\ 0 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}\) and \(\begin{pmatrix} -2 \\ 6 \\ -3 \end{pmatrix}\)?
classified_questions.label: f

classified_questions.question: How would you find the closest point on the line to the point \((2,3,4)\)?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: Can you explain why the vector pointing from \((2,3,4)\) to the point on the line must be orthogonal to the direction vector of the line?
classified_questions.label: r

classified_questions.question: What is the dot product between the vector \(\begin{pmatrix} 2 - 2t \\ -3 + 6t \\ -3 - 3t \end{pmatrix}\) and the direction vector of the line \(\begin{pmatrix} -2 \\ 6 \\ -3 \end{pmatrix}\)?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: Solve the equation \((2 - 2t)(-2) + (-3 + 6t)(6) + (-3 - 3t)(-3) = 0\) for \(t\). What is the value of \(t\)?
classified_questions.label: b

classified_questions.question: What are the coordinates of the closest point on the line to \((2,3,4)\)?
classified_questions.label: b
